target.expr: {host="wags-ai", unit="ollama.service"} |~ "(?i)(load model|llm load progress|llama_model_load|new model load)"

description: Live view of the wags-ai AI workstation (192.168.2.240, RTX 5090, bare-metal Ollama). Built so natural VRAM thrash and model lifecycle events are obvious without staging.

panels.title: VRAM by model (stacked) — watch this for eviction cycles
panels.description: Stacked area showing how much VRAM each loaded model holds. When the stack collapses and a new layer appears, a model was evicted to load another — the AI-justifies-AI thrash signature.

panels.title: Currently loaded models
panels.description: Sorted by VRAM resident. Expires_in is seconds until Ollama would idle-evict it (negative = stale).

panels.title: Loaded model count over time
panels.description: A flat line is steady state. Dips toward 1 are eviction events — the thrash signal.

targets.expr: {host="wags-ai", unit="ollama.service"} |~ "(?i)(load|unload|llm load progress|stopped server|llama_model|free memory|removing model|error|panic)"

panels.title: Per-process GPU memory (who else holds VRAM)
HTTP API request rate by endpoint
panels.description: Derived from Ollama GIN access logs. Spike on /api/chat means actual inference traffic; spikes on /api/ps mean health-check pressure.

panels.title: HTTP API p95 latency by endpoint
panels.description: Server-side latency that Sigil/Vercel SDK can't see — Ollama's own measurement of each request.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {host="wags-ai", unit="ollama.service"} |~ `\[GIN\]` | pattern `<_> | <status> | <duration> | <_> | <_> <path>` | unwrap duration(duration) [$__rate_interval]) by (path)

panels.title: HTTP status codes
panels.description: 5xx = Ollama internal error. 4xx = bad request (e.g., model not loaded, wrong shape). Watch the 5xx line — it should be flat at zero.

panels.title: GPU clock-event reasons (throttle state)
panels.description: Lights up when the GPU is throttled for any reason. hw_thermal / hw_power_brake = real problems. gpu_idle = expected when no workload.

panels.title: Per-process GPU memory (ollama runners + others)
panels.description: Each Ollama runner subprocess holds one model. Watch for runner spawns/exits — they correlate with model load/unload.

panels.title: Disk read throughput (model-load cost)
panels.description: When Ollama loads a model from disk, this spikes. Cold loads = several hundred MB/s for a few seconds.

panels.title: ECC + driver-reserved memory
panels.description: Volatile ECC error counter (should stay flat). Driver-reserved is overhead that's not available for models.

panels.title: Model architecture & capabilities (Sigil can't see this)
panels.description: From /api/show. Capabilities tell you which models support tool calls, vision, or embeddings. Context length / vocab / parameter count are what Sigil's prompt-token metrics imply but never reveal directly.

targets.expr: max by (model) (ollama_model_capability{host="wags-ai", capability="tools"}) or on() (max by (model) (ollama_model_capability{host="wags-ai"}) * 0)
max by (model) (ollama_model_capability{host="wags-ai", capability="vision"}) or on() (max by (model) (ollama_model_capability{host="wags-ai"}) * 0)

title: AI Obs — wags-ai (.240 RTX 5090 + Ollama)
